name: Hero CTAs on /payments deeplink into the assistant with a pre-filled prompt
description: The 'Record a payment' and 'Export this month' buttons in the /payments hero used to be decorative. They now anchor to /assistant?seed=<prompt>. AsstChat reads the seed param, pre-fills the composer with a starter line, focuses the textarea, and strips the param from the URL so a refresh doesn't re-seed.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: Just got cash from a client; wants to log it without typing the framing sentence himself.

steps.action: Clicks 'Record a payment' in the hero
AsstChat mounts and reads the seed param
steps.expect: Composer textarea is pre-filled with 'Record a payment I just received.'; textarea has focus; URL is now /assistant (param stripped via history.replaceState)
No re-seed (the URL no longer carries the param); textarea state is whatever localStorage held, NOT the seed

steps.action: Returns to /payments and clicks 'Export this month'
steps.expect: Same behavior with seed='Export this month's payments as a CSV.'; user can edit before sending

evaluationCriteria.check: The CTAs render visually identical to their original button form (text-decoration:none on .pph__cta and .pph__ghost)
The deeplink feels intentional — the user lands in the right surface with the right sentence ready to send
useEffect runs once on mount; seed handling is idempotent across navigation
Anchors carry the same accessible name as the original buttons; keyboard Enter activates them
